verbs.name: adopt
verbs.short: Record a handed-over recipe this repository builds from — the reader-side seal

flags.name: root
flags.usage: repository that adopts the recipe

flags.name: source
flags.usage: where pull re-fetches this recipe from later — a path or an http(s) URL (default: the file argument)

verbs.name: conform
verbs.short: Check an output file against a recipe's exact-bound contracts

flags.name: contract
flags.usage: check against one exact contract by its index (default: all)

flags.name: root
flags.usage: project root (searched for the recipe when a slug is given)

verbs.name: docs
verbs.short: Print this binary's command surface — the source the docs page is built from

flags.name: json
flags.usage: emit the surface as JSON (what the docs page consumes)

flags.name: out
flags.usage: write the JSON snapshot to this file (used by `go generate`)

verbs.name: export
verbs.short: Write one recipe (or, with --bundle, a composed set) as a single self-contained file

flags.name: bundle
flags.usage: treat <slug> as a bundle manifest and compose its members into one document

flags.name: root
flags.usage: project root (searched for this repo's own recipes before the official corpus)

flags.name: stdout
flags.usage: print the composed document to stdout instead of writing a file (for piping)

verbs.name: feedback
verbs.short: File and list report-backs — the channel a recipe's next version comes from

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.short: Validate a reader's report-back against the protocol and file it (`-` reads stdin)

flags.name: root
flags.usage: project root (the recipe this report answers must be authored here)

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.short: List the filed report-backs, per recipe

flags.name: root
flags.usage: project root

verbs.name: genre
verbs.short: Print the recipe genre spec — the authoring rules this binary enforces

flags.name: version
flags.usage: print only the embedded genre spec version

verbs.name: harvest
verbs.short: Gather a recipe's raw material from a revision range of the project's own record

flags.name: out
flags.usage: write the harvest here (default: stdout)

flags.name: root
flags.usage: repository to harvest

flags.name: since
flags.usage: starting revision, exclusive (a tag: the release before the work)

flags.name: until
flags.usage: ending revision, inclusive

verbs.name: init
verbs.short: Install the recipe-authoring surface into this repository (skill, AGENTS.md block, seeds)

flags.name: root
flags.usage: repository to initialize

verbs.name: lint
verbs.short: Check a recipe corpus against the genre — shape, acceptance, scars, and neutrality

flags.name: root
flags.usage: project root (its config names the recipes home and the forbidden product vocabulary)

verbs.name: list
verbs.short: List the recipes available here — this project's own, and the official corpus

flags.name: root
flags.usage: project root (its own recipes are listed alongside the official corpus)

verbs.name: new
verbs.short: Scaffold a draft recipe — coached section stubs, optionally pre-seeded from a harvest

flags.name: from-harvest
flags.usage: a `sporo harvest --out` file; its scar candidates pre-seed the scars section

flags.name: root
flags.usage: project root (the draft lands in its recipes home)

flags.name: title
flags.usage: the recipe's title (defaults to a TODO)

verbs.name: projects
verbs.short: List the repositories on this machine that sporo was installed into

verbs.name: publish
verbs.short: Verify a recipe is sealed and gate-passed, then open a corpus PR

flags.name: check
flags.usage: verify only (the CI/server semantic): print the one-line result and exit

flags.name: root
flags.usage: project root

verbs.name: pull
verbs.short: Check adopted recipes against their sources — loud when an exact contract moved

flags.name: apply
flags.usage: refresh the stored copy and record for each update (default: report only)

flags.name: root
flags.usage: repository whose adopted recipes are checked

verbs.name: recipes
verbs.short: List every sealed recipe across the repos on this machine — id, version, and where

flags.name: adopted
flags.usage: list recipes this machine pulled IN (the reader-side ledger), not the ones it authored

flags.name: all
flags.usage: include unsealed and draft recipes (scans each project's home, not just its registry)

flags.name: json
flags.usage: emit the fleet index as JSON for scripting

verbs.name: review
verbs.short: Build a self-contained review pack for any agent, and verify the verdicts it returns

flags.name: root
flags.usage: project root

subcommands.name: verify
subcommands.short: Validate returned verdicts and record the tally beside the recipe's seal

flags.name: root
flags.usage: project root

verbs.name: seal
verbs.short: Record a recipe's version and content hash in the registry — a sealed recipe never silently mutates

flags.name: root
flags.usage: project root (its registry records the seal)

verbs.name: seed
verbs.short: Author, check, seal and export install seeds — the recipe verbs, one kind over

subcommands.name: export
subcommands.short: Write one seed as a single self-contained install file — runner preamble first, then the seed

flags.name: root
flags.usage: project root (searched for this repo's own seeds before the embedded corpus)

flags.name: stdout
flags.usage: print the composed document to stdout instead of writing a file (for piping)

subcommands.name: lint
subcommands.short: Check this project's seed corpus against the genre — shape, acceptance, neutrality, seals

flags.name: root
flags.usage: project root (its config names the seed home and the forbidden product vocabulary)

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.short: List the seeds available here — this project's own, and the embedded corpus

flags.name: root
flags.usage: project root (its own seeds are listed alongside the embedded corpus)

subcommands.name: new
subcommands.short: Scaffold a draft seed — coached section stubs, born a draft

flags.name: root
flags.usage: project root (the draft lands in its seed home)

flags.name: title
flags.usage: the seed's title (defaults to a TODO)

subcommands.name: seal
subcommands.short: Record a seed's version and content hash in the registry — a sealed seed never silently mutates

flags.name: root
flags.usage: project root (its registry records the seal)

verbs.name: update
verbs.short: Re-sync the managed authoring surface from this binary — never clobbering your edits

flags.name: root
flags.usage: repository to update

verbs.name: upgrade
verbs.short: Update this binary to the latest release — then run `sporo update` in each repo

flags.name: check
flags.usage: report the latest release and its notes; change nothing
